# Continuity and Differentiability - DETAILED NOTES

## Introduction

Continuity and differentiability are fundamental concepts in calculus that help us understand how functions behave. In real-life applications, these concepts are crucial in physics, engineering, and economics to model and analyze complex phenomena. For instance, in physics, the concept of continuity is used to describe the motion of objects, while differentiability helps in determining the rate of change of physical quantities like velocity and acceleration.

In this chapter, we will delve into the world of continuity and differentiability, exploring the underlying theories, concepts, and applications.

## What is Continuity?

Continuity is a concept that describes how a function behaves at a point or in a region. A function is said to be continuous at a point if it can be drawn without lifting the pencil from the paper. In other words, if a function is continuous at a point, it means that the function's value at that point is equal to the limit of the function as the input (or independent variable) approaches that point.

### Definition

A function f(x) is said to be continuous at a point x = a if the following conditions are satisfied:

* The function is defined at x = a
* The limit of the function as x approaches a exists
* The limit of the function as x approaches a is equal to the value of the function at x = a

### Characteristics

* A function is continuous at all points where it is defined
* A function can be continuous at a point even if its derivative is not defined at that point

## Components / Sub-topics

### 1. Continuity at a Point

| Type | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| Discontinuity | A function is said to be discontinuous at a point if it is not continuous at that point. |
| Infinite Discontinuity | A function is said to have an infinite discontinuity at a point if the limit of the function as x approaches that point is infinity. |
| Jump Discontinuity | A function is said to have a jump discontinuity at a point if the left-hand limit and right-hand limit of the function at that point are unequal. |

### 2. Continuity in an Interval

| Type | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| Continuity in an Interval | A function is said to be continuous in an interval if it is continuous at every point in that interval. |
| Discontinuity in an Interval | A function is said to be discontinuous in an interval if it is not continuous at any point in that interval. |

## Core Concept 1: Differentiability

Differentiability is a concept that describes how a function changes with respect to a small change in its input. A function is said to be differentiable at a point if it can be approximated by a linear function in the neighborhood of that point.

### Definition

A function f(x) is said to be differentiable at a point x = a if the limit of the difference quotient as h approaches 0 exists.

### Characteristics

* A function can be differentiable at a point even if it is not continuous at that point
* A function can be differentiable at a point if its derivative is defined at that point

## Core Concept 2: Differentiation Rules

There are several differentiation rules that help us find the derivative of a function. Some of the most important rules are:

* Sum Rule: If f(x) and g(x) are two functions, then the derivative of f(x) + g(x) is f'(x) + g'(x)
* Product Rule: If f(x) and g(x) are two functions, then the derivative of f(x)g(x) is f'(x)g(x) + f(x)g'(x)
* Quotient Rule: If f(x) and g(x) are two functions, then the derivative of f(x)/g(x) is (f'(x)g(x) - f(x)g'(x)) / (g(x))^2

## Ideal and Non-Ideal Scenarios

* A function can be continuous at a point but not differentiable at that point
* A function can be differentiable at a point but not continuous at that point
* A function can be both continuous and differentiable at a point

## Properties & Applications

* Continuity and differentiability are essential properties of functions in calculus
* These concepts have numerous applications in physics, engineering, economics, and computer science
* Continuity and differentiability are used to model and analyze real-world phenomena like motion, vibration, and population growth

### Important Theorems

| Theorem | Statement |
| --- | --- |
| Rolle's Theorem | If a function is continuous in a closed interval and differentiable in an open interval, then there exists a point in the open interval where the derivative of the function is zero. |
| Mean Value Theorem | If a function is continuous in a closed interval and differentiable in an open interval, then there exists a point in the open interval where the derivative of the function is equal to the average rate of change of the function in the closed interval. |

### Important Exam Points

* Continuity and differentiability are two fundamental concepts in calculus
* A function can be continuous at a point but not differentiable at that point
* A function can be differentiable at a point but not continuous at that point
* Continuity and differentiability have numerous applications in physics, engineering, economics, and computer science.
